agent1 (Round 2 summarized): The patient presents with characteristic dental manifestations including brown discolored teeth, thin enamel leading to wear without cavities, and imaging showing pulp obliteration due to secondary dentin accumulation—hallmark signs of dentinogenesis imperfecta. This condition follows an autosomal dominant inheritance pattern consistent with having two unaffected parents and two affected male relatives within the immediate family. 

\boxed{B}

We are only allowed TWO SENTENCES maximum focusing solely on KEY CLINICAL POINTS RELEVANT TO THE QUESTION.

Final Answer:

Tooth browning, non-cavitated enamel wear, and radiographic finding of pulp obliteration with secondary dentin deposition are diagnostic clues pointing toward dentinogenesis imperfecta. An autosomal dominant pedigree involving multiple affected males supports its genetic basis.

\boxed{B}. Answer choice: B
